Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class AlternativeOrderCreate

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.AlternativeOrderCreate
public class AlternativeOrderCreate extends Object
AlternativeOrderCreate

An order for an alternative investment.

  • Constructor Details

    • AlternativeOrderCreate

      public AlternativeOrderCreate(String clientOrderId, Optional<String> currencyCode, Optional<? extends DistributionType> distributionType, Optional<Boolean> full, String identifier, AlternativeOrderCreateIdentifierType identifierType, Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> notionalValue, Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> quantity, AlternativeOrderCreateSide side)

    • AlternativeOrderCreate

      public AlternativeOrderCreate(String clientOrderId, String identifier, AlternativeOrderCreateIdentifierType identifierType, AlternativeOrderCreateSide side)

  • Method Details

    • clientOrderId

      public String clientOrderId()
      A client-supplied unique ID to enforce idempotency. Each ID must be unique within the account.

      If a create request includes a `client_order_id` that matches an existing order’s `client_order_id`, the service will respond with `ALREADY_EXISTS` and will not create a new order resource.

    • currencyCode

      public Optional<String> currencyCode()
      The currency code of the order. Supports only "USD" and will default to "USD" if unspecified in the request.

    • distributionType

      public Optional<DistributionType> distributionType()
      Identifies the distribution type for buy side orders (that is, when `side` value = `BUY`). - Orders will be rejected if the alternative investment asset does not allow the requested distribution type. - Not relevant for sell orders (that is, when `side` value = `SELL`). - Confirm the asset’s `cash_distribution_allowed` and `reinvestment_distribution_allowed` properties to know what it allows.

    • full

      public Optional<Boolean> full()
      Indicates whether this order is for a full redemption (as in, all shares redeemed). - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Note that the issuer may allow only partial redemption. - Not relevant to subscription orders.

    • identifier

      public String identifier()
      The asset identifier value, dependent on enum value set for `identifier_type`.

      For example, if `identifier_type` = `ASSET_ID`, then the value for `identifier` is the `asset_id` value.

    • identifierType

      public AlternativeOrderCreateIdentifierType identifierType()
      Identifies which parameter value is used to identify the asset in the order. The value for that parameter is used in the `identifier` field.

    • notionalValue

      public Optional<DecimalCreate> notionalValue()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • quantity

      public Optional<DecimalCreate> quantity()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • side

      public AlternativeOrderCreateSide side()
      The side of the order.

    • builder

      public static AlternativeOrderCreate.Builder builder()

    • withClientOrderId

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withClientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      A client-supplied unique ID to enforce idempotency. Each ID must be unique within the account.

      If a create request includes a `client_order_id` that matches an existing order’s `client_order_id`, the service will respond with `ALREADY_EXISTS` and will not create a new order resource.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withCurrencyCode(String currencyCode)
      The currency code of the order. Supports only "USD" and will default to "USD" if unspecified in the request.

    • withCurrencyCode

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withCurrencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      The currency code of the order. Supports only "USD" and will default to "USD" if unspecified in the request.

    • withDistributionType

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withDistributionType(DistributionType distributionType)
      Identifies the distribution type for buy side orders (that is, when `side` value = `BUY`). - Orders will be rejected if the alternative investment asset does not allow the requested distribution type. - Not relevant for sell orders (that is, when `side` value = `SELL`). - Confirm the asset’s `cash_distribution_allowed` and `reinvestment_distribution_allowed` properties to know what it allows.

    • withDistributionType

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withDistributionType(Optional<? extends DistributionType> distributionType)
      Identifies the distribution type for buy side orders (that is, when `side` value = `BUY`). - Orders will be rejected if the alternative investment asset does not allow the requested distribution type. - Not relevant for sell orders (that is, when `side` value = `SELL`). - Confirm the asset’s `cash_distribution_allowed` and `reinvestment_distribution_allowed` properties to know what it allows.

    • withFull

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withFull(boolean full)
      Indicates whether this order is for a full redemption (as in, all shares redeemed). - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Note that the issuer may allow only partial redemption. - Not relevant to subscription orders.

    • withFull

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withFull(Optional<Boolean> full)
      Indicates whether this order is for a full redemption (as in, all shares redeemed). - Orders can specify only one of `full`, `notional_value`, or `quantity`. - Note that the issuer may allow only partial redemption. - Not relevant to subscription orders.

    • withIdentifier

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withIdentifier(String identifier)
      The asset identifier value, dependent on enum value set for `identifier_type`.

      For example, if `identifier_type` = `ASSET_ID`, then the value for `identifier` is the `asset_id` value.

    • withIdentifierType

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withIdentifierType(AlternativeOrderCreateIdentifierType identifierType)
      Identifies which parameter value is used to identify the asset in the order. The value for that parameter is used in the `identifier` field.

    • withNotionalValue

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withNotionalValue(DecimalCreate notionalValue)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • withNotionalValue

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withNotionalValue(Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> notionalValue)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • withQuantity

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withQuantity(DecimalCreate quantity)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • withQuantity

      public AlternativeOrderCreate withQuantity(Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> quantity)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html
